Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(3 inline comments)

sanityn test 60 failed as follows:

 == sanityn test 60: Verify data_version behaviour == 07:06:33 (1352127993)
 ++ lustre_version_code mds1
 ++ '[' -z mds1 ']'
 +++ lustre_build_version mds1
 +++ awk '/ version: / { print $3; exit; }'
 +++ do_facet mds1 /usr/sbin/lctl lustre_build_version
 +++ sed -e 's/^v//' -e 's/-.*//'
 ++ version_code jenkins
 +++ tr '[:punct:]' ' '
 ++ eval set -- jenkins
 +++ set -- jenkins
 /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh: line 341: (jenkins << 16) | ( << 8) | : syntax error: operand expected (error token is "<< 8) | ")

Please look into the comment in test-framework.sh for more test results.
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

the only concern is that lctl itself does not verify modules, so in theory it's possible to run lctl with old modules which do not support OIscrub.

Patch Set 4:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/10865/

Patch Set 4: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/10865/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 4:

Alex,
The "lctl get_param version" command will actually get the version string from the /proc/sys/lustre/version file from the kernel, if it is available. If not, then it will be gotten from the lctl tool itself. While in theory this could cause inconsistency in strange environments, for all of our test configurations the lctl and modules will match.

Patch Set 4:

yes, I understand that in our testing environment it should be doing fine, so i set +1 :)

parents.subject: LU-6387 build: lustre-dev debain package missing shared libraries

commit.subject: LU-790 tests: clean up lustre version checking
commit.message:
LU-790 tests: clean up lustre version checking

Since commit 48bad5d9db9baa7bca093de5c54294adf1cf8303, the check in
sanity-scrub.sh to verify if the MDS had a new enough Lustre version
was being run before modules are loaded, if sanity-scrub.sh was run
stand-alone.  In addition to using "lctl get_param version" to get
the version, use "lctl lustre_build_version" or "lctl --version",
which will always work, even if the modules are not loaded on the
node.  This also avoids the need to load the modules just to find
that the test should not be run.

While cleaning up this code, move the lustre_version_code() earlier in
test-framework.sh, so that it is declared before calling functions,
and is co-located with the other version checking code.  Not strictly
necessary for bash scripts, but nicer when reading the code.

Fix test regressions in sanity.sh test_48[ab] since commit 13b269ab77
that incorrectly used a full path for test_mkdir() opertations instead
of using a relative filename as the test originally intended.

Also remove the obsolete check_kernel_version() code, which was only
used when we had a patched client kernel, which is no longer the case,
and remove a few overly verbose logging lines from tests.

Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Change-Id: I8a8aec11370d395dcb4f4b250001ecdb40500c1e
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/4173
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Emoly Liu <emoly.liu@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Nunez <james.a.nunez@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
